steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The phrase before the blank, “the observatory sites...number nearly five [blank] across the globe,” is an independent clause. After the comma, “the radars track ionospheric irregularities and other geospace phenomena” is another independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Choice A correctly uses a period to separate two independent clauses, placing it after “dozen” so the next sentence begins with “Located across the globe” describing what the radars do.

Choices B, C, and D all fail to create a proper sentence boundary.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
